metadata.purpose: Business glossary for ecommerce SQL/RAG agent metric definitions
metadata.design_principle: mart/view-first semantic layer; use base tables only when no suitable mart/view exists.
source_priority: 2. fallback_sources: base tables or lower-level views when mart/view cannot support the metric
3. calculability_status: mark metrics as not_directly_calculable if required source data is missing

known_project_sources.mart_order_summary: order-level view; 1 row per order; useful for AOV, revenue, cost, gross profit, margin, return and delivery metrics
known_project_sources.mart_daily_sales: daily sales view; useful for revenue trend, order count, item count and daily KPI aggregation
known_project_sources.mart_product_sales: product/category-level sales view; useful for product revenue, product/category AOV, margin and product performance
known_project_sources.mart_user_segment: user/customer-level segment view; useful for customer segments such as new, returning, inactive, VIP, whale and retention/churn logic if fields exist
known_project_sources.events: base event table; useful for traffic/funnel metrics because event-level mart may not exist yet
known_project_sources.orders: base order table; fallback for lifecycle and fulfillment date logic
known_project_sources.order_items: base item table; fallback for item-level revenue and product joins
known_project_sources.products: base product table; fallback for product cost, retail price and category fields
known_project_sources.inventory_items: base inventory table; fallback for stock availability logic if current-stock fields exist

notes: sql_formula may use logical aliases such as order_revenue, gross_profit, total_sessions, converted_sessions, churned_users, or eligible_users.
preferred_source_fields shows the expected fields from the preferred mart/view. Validate actual column names in SQLite before production SQL generation.
Rate metrics are expressed as percentages unless otherwise stated, so formulas use numerator * 100.0 / denominator.
If the preferred_source does not contain the needed field, use fallback_sources.

churn_rate.definition: Percentage of customers who previously purchased but have no completed orders for more than the churn inactivity window.
business_logic: eligible_users = users with at least one completed order before the evaluation date
churned_users = eligible_users whose latest completed order is older than 90 or 180 days
completed order filter: status = 'Complete'
If mart_user_segment already has inactive/churn segment, use that instead of recalculating from orders

retention_rate.definition: Percentage of existing customers who made at least one completed repeat purchase within the target period.
business_logic: eligible_existing_users = users who purchased before the target period
retained_users = eligible_existing_users with at least one completed order during the target period
completed order filter: status = 'Complete'

reactivation_rate.definition: Percentage of inactive customers who returned and made a completed purchase during the target period.
business_logic: inactive_users = users with no completed orders for more than 90 or 180 days before the target period
reactivated_users = inactive_users with a completed order during the target period

roi.definition: Profit generated relative to cost. In this project this is product/order profitability ROI, not paid-media ROI.
business_logic: gross_profit = order_revenue - order_cost
order_cost = sum product cost for sold items
Use completed orders when analyzing realized revenue/profit
Use mart_order_summary because it has one row per order and avoids duplicated order counts from order_items
completed order filter: status = 'Complete'

conversion_rate.definition: Percentage of visitors or sessions that resulted in a completed purchase.
business_logic: No traffic/funnel mart is assumed yet, so events is the correct source
total_sessions = distinct events.session_id
converted_sessions = sessions that directly contain a successful purchase event (e.g., event_type = 'purchase') or are explicitly mapped to a completed order_id within the same session
completed order filter: status = 'Complete'

ltv_clv.definition: Estimated total value generated by a customer over their relationship with the business.
business_logic: AOV = completed order revenue / completed orders
average_purchase_frequency_per_year = completed orders per customer per year
average_customer_lifetime_years = average active lifetime in years
If mart_user_segment stores customer_lifetime_revenue, use it for historical CLV

repeat_purchase_rate.definition: Percentage of customers who made more than one completed purchase.
business_logic: repeat_customers = users with more than one completed order
total_customers = users with at least one completed order

new_vs_returning_customer_ratio.definition: Ratio of new customers to returning customers in the target period.
business_logic: new_customer = first completed order occurs within target period
returning_customer = customer had completed order before target period and/or completed_order_count > 1

vip_whale_ratio.definition: Percentage of customers classified as high-value customers.
business_logic: vip_customers = customers in top revenue percentile, usually top 5% or top 10%
whale_customers = customers in top 1% by lifetime revenue

gross_margin_rate.definition: Percentage of revenue remaining after product cost.
business_logic: gross_profit = order_revenue - order_cost
Use mart_order_summary if margin is analyzed at order level
Use mart_product_sales if margin is analyzed by product/category

discount_rate.definition: Percentage discount relative to original retail price. Calculated based on purchased items in order_items joined with products.
business_logic: Use only if products.retail_price represents original/list price
join order_items.product_id = products.id

return_rate.definition: Percentage of orders returned by customers.
business_logic: returned_orders = orders with status = 'Returned' or returned_at/order_returned_at IS NOT NULL

refund_rate.definition: Percentage of orders or order items refunded.
business_logic: Use only if refund status, refund amount, or refund table exists
refund_rate.notes: If the database only has returned orders but no refund data, use return_rate instead.

on_time_delivery_rate.definition: Percentage of delivered orders delivered within the target delivery SLA.
business_logic: delivered_orders = orders with delivered_at/order_delivered_at IS NOT NULL
on_time_orders = delivered orders where delivery_days <= target_sla_days

product_view_rate.definition: Percentage of sessions that included at least one product page view.
business_logic: product_view_sessions = sessions with event_type indicating product view or product page URI

add_to_cart_rate.definition: Percentage of product-view sessions that added at least one product to cart.
business_logic: add_to_cart_sessions = sessions with add_to_cart event

initiate_checkout_rate.definition: Percentage of cart sessions that initiated checkout.

checkout_to_purchase_rate.definition: Percentage of checkout sessions that completed purchase. Purchase_sessions must be a subset of checkout_sessions
business_logic: purchase_sessions should be tied to completed orders where possible

cart_abandonment_rate.definition: Percentage of add-to-cart sessions that did not complete purchase.
business_logic: cart_sessions = sessions with add_to_cart event
purchase_sessions = cart_sessions that later resulted in completed purchase

search_usage_rate.definition: Percentage of sessions that used onsite search.
business_logic: search_sessions = sessions with search event or search query URI pattern

search_cvr.definition: Percentage of onsite search sessions that resulted in completed purchase.

traffic_metrics.definition: Website/app traffic metrics based on sessions, users, events and traffic source.

roas.definition: Revenue generated per dollar of advertising spend.
business_logic: Only use this metric if an ad spend or campaign spend table exists

cac.definition: Average cost to acquire one new customer.
business_logic: Only use this metric if marketing spend or acquisition cost data exists

cpa_cpp.business_name: Cost Per Acquisition / Cost Per Purchase
cpa_cpp.definition: Average marketing cost per acquired customer or completed purchase.
business_logic: Only use this metric if marketing cost data exists

ctr.definition: Percentage of impressions that resulted in clicks.
business_logic: Use only if event_type can clearly identify impression and click events

stockout_rate.definition: Percentage of products unavailable for purchase.
business_logic: Use inventory availability logic only if inventory table represents current stock
If inventory_items is historical inventory, this metric may require a separate stock snapshot table

nps.definition: Customer loyalty score based on survey responses.
business_logic: promoters = score 9-10
passives = score 7-8
detractors = score 0-6

revenue.definition: Total sales revenue generated from order items.
business_logic: Revenue is calculated from sale_price at order item level
Use mart_order_summary.order_revenue when available
Fallback to SUM(order_items.sale_price) if mart_order_summary is unavailable

new_customers.definition: Number of customers classified as new customers based on customer segment logic.
business_logic: Use mart_user_segment where customer_segment = 'New Customers'
Fallback logic: users whose first completed order date falls within the new customer window
completed order filter: status = 'Complete'

returned_orders.definition: Number of orders that were returned.
business_logic: Returned orders are identified where order_returned_at IS NOT NULL
Fallback logic: returned_at IS NOT NULL from orders
Status can be used as secondary validation if status = 'Returned'

delivery_time.definition: Average number of days between order creation and order delivery.
business_logic: Only include delivered orders where delivered_at is not null
Delivery time is measured in days from order creation to delivery
Exclude cancelled or undelivered orders from delivery time calculation

returning_customers.definition: Number of customers who have more than one completed order.
business_logic: Use mart_user_segment where completed_order_count > 1
Fallback logic: users with COUNT(DISTINCT completed order_id) > 1
completed order filter: status = 'Complete'

time_periods.last_month: DATE({date_field}) >= DATE('now', 'start of month', '-1 month') AND DATE({date_field}) < DATE('now', 'start of month')
time_periods.month_to_date: DATE({date_field}) >= DATE('now', 'start of month') AND DATE({date_field}) <= DATE('now')
time_periods.year_to_date: DATE({date_field}) >= DATE('now', 'start of year') AND DATE({date_field}) <= DATE('now')

new_customer.definition: Customer whose first completed order occurred within the target period.

returning_customer.definition: Customer with more than one completed order.

inactive_customer_90d.definition: Customer with no completed orders in the last 90 days.

inactive_customer_180d.definition: Customer with no completed orders in the last 180 days.

reactivated_customer.definition: Previously inactive customer who completed a new order in the target period.
reactivated_customer.logic: inactive before period_start AND completed order during target period

vip_customer.definition: High-value customer based on lifetime revenue percentile.
vip_customer.logic: customer_lifetime_revenue in top 10% or custom business threshold

whale_customer.definition: Extremely high-value customer based on lifetime revenue percentile.
whale_customer.logic: customer_lifetime_revenue in top 1% or custom business threshold

traffic_metrics.definition: Traffic metrics should use events as the current source until a mart_traffic_funnel view is created.
